Title: The Wor1-like Protein Fgp1 Regulates Pathogenicity, Toxin Synthesis and Reproduction in the Phytopathogenic Fungus Fusarium graminearum

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fungal virulence factor during wheat head infection; mycotoxin harmful to crop quality and food/feed safety
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Wor1-like GTI1/PAC2 family protein with WOPRa/WOPRb (GTI1/PAC2) DNA-binding domain; glutamine-rich divergent C-terminus
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for trichothecene accumulation, pathogenic spread through wheat heads, normal conidiation, and efficient ascospore formation; deletion abolishes DON and 15-ADON accumulation in planta and in vitro
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of FGP1 abolished trichothecene accumulation in infected wheat heads and in putrescine medium. 2. Δfgp1 strains failed to spread beyond the inoculated spikelet/rachis node and showed strongly reduced pathogenicity. 3. FGP1 positively regulated TRI cluster expression and many infection- and secondary metabolism-associated genes, while negatively regulating some other secondary metabolite genes such as the aurofusarin cluster. 4. FGP1 also contributed to conidiation, ascospore production, and toxin-associated hyphal morphology.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; complementation with native FGP1; wheat point inoculation; toxin quantification (DON/15-ADON/3-ADON); northern blot for TRI14; microarray transcriptomics; microscopy of infection and hyphal morphology
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression level itself was not reported to differ significantly across tested conditions; function was most evident during putrescine-induced toxin production, wheat infection, and sporulation-related conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: putrescine/polyamine toxin-inducing medium; wheat head infection; complete medium; CMC and mung bean agar for sporulation; carrot agar for sexual development
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics; PlexDB accessions NF2 (wheat infection), NF3 (growth on complete medium), FG18 (growth on putrescine medium)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FGP1 is the key Wor1-clade ortholog in F. graminearum; its C-terminal region is highly divergent and species-specific. Chimeric swap with F. oxysporum SGE1 caused partial loss of function, indicating species-adapted regulatory specificity. Discussion notes possible upstream regulation by StuA and possible phosphorylation but not directly tested here.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes severe loss of virulence and reproductive defects; no major vegetative growth defect was observed
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for reducing Fusarium head blight and trichothecene contamination by interfering with a major regulator of toxin synthesis and pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native FGP1 promoter in complementation/chimeric constructs; hygromycin resistance marker driven by constitutive GPD1 promoter and TRPC terminator in knockout cassette

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pac2-like GTI1/PAC2 family protein with WOPRa/WOPRb domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not required for pathogenicity or trichothecene accumulation under the conditions tested
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FGP2 is the PAC2-clade paralog in F. graminearum. 2. Δfgp2 mutants retained near-wild-type wheat head pathogenicity. 3. Δfgp2 mutants accumulated DON and 15-ADON at levels comparable to wild type.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; wheat point inoculation; toxin quantification in infected spikelets
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested comparison gene showing that the PAC2-clade homolog is dispensable for toxin accumulation and virulence in this system
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evident pathogenicity or toxin-production defect upon deletion under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low value as target for controlling trichothecene contamination based on this study
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Hygromycin resistance marker driven by constitutive GPD1 promoter and TRPC terminator in knockout cassette

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: infected wheat spikelets; toxin-inducing liquid culture
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes fungal infection spread in wheat and contaminates grain with harmful mycotoxins
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream regulator required for TRI gene expression and trichothecene production in planta and in vitro; acts as a major switch coupling pathogenic development with toxin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FGP1 is absolutely required for DON and 15-ADON production during wheat infection and in putrescine medium. 2. Δfgp1 abolishes or strongly reduces expression of the TRI biosynthetic cluster and additional toxin-associated genes. 3. FGP1 positively regulates TRI6 and TRI10, suggesting upstream control over the trichothecene regulatory module. 4. FGP1 also regulates other secondary metabolism clusters, sporulation, sexual reproduction, and toxin-associated bulbous hyphal morphology.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes trichothecene biosynthesis and pathogenic spread; deletion eliminates detectable toxin accumulation and blocks spread beyond the rachis node
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TRI cluster genes including TRI6 (FGSG_03536), TRI10, TRI14, FGSG_03534, FGSG_03537, FGSG_03538, FGSG_03540, FGSG_03543; other toxin-associated genes FGSG_00007; FGSG_01819; FGSG_07562; FGSG_10397; butenolide cluster genes during infection; NPS8 cluster; aurofusarin cluster (negative regulation)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/positively regulates trichothecene biosynthetic and associated genes; positively regulates butenolide and NPS8 cluster genes; represses aurofusarin cluster genes under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary metabolite biosynthesis; specifically trichothecene biosynthesis and broader regulation of fungal secondary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation, primarily positive activation of biosynthetic gene expression and negative regulation of selected secondary metabolite clusters
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: putrescine/polyamine toxin-inducing conditions; plant infection context; possible upstream influence of StuA and kinase signaling discussed but not directly demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Wor1-like GTI1/PAC2 family protein containing WOPRa and WOPRb DNA-binding boxes; divergent glutamine-rich C-terminus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion and complementation; northern blot of TRI14; microarray transcriptomics in putrescine medium and infected wheat; toxin measurements; infection microscopy; chimeric domain-swap complementation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No significant condition-dependent expression changes reported across tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional effects evident during putrescine-induced toxin production, wheat infection, conidiation, and sexual development
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: putrescine medium; wheat head infection; complete medium; CMC; mung bean agar; carrot agar
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native FGP1 promoter in complementation/chimeric constructs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics; PlexDB accessions NF2, NF3, FG18
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No direct epigenetic mechanism shown; discussion notes phenotypic similarity to heterochromatin protein mutant hep1, suggesting possible indirect connection to chromatin regulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of FGP1 reduces conidia and ascospore production but has little effect on vegetative growth
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value antifungal intervention target to reduce both Fusarium head blight spread and trichothecene contamination; species specificity of the C-terminus should be considered when designing inhibitors or comparative functional studies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous SGE1 from F. oxysporum could not complement Δfgp1 in F. graminearum, and an FGP1/SGE1 chimera only partially restored function, showing species-specific regulatory adaptation. The conserved threonine in WOPRa is a putative phosphorylation site based on family knowledge, but not experimentally tested here.
